cards.example: Hôm nay em mệt quá, em muốn ngủ sớm.
cards.exampleMeaning: I am very tired today, I want to sleep early.
cards.brokenCue: Short home phrase. Good when you want to say how you feel quickly.
cards.notes: Use naturally at home. You do not need to overbuild the sentence.

cards.example: Anh đói rồi, mình ăn gì đây?
cards.exampleMeaning: I am hungry now, what should we eat?
cards.brokenCue: Very common. Good for daily life and easy to remember.
cards.notes: You can swap anh or em depending on who is speaking.

cards.example: Đợi em một chút, em đang thay đồ.
cards.exampleMeaning: Wait for me a moment, I am changing clothes.
cards.brokenCue: Useful when moving around the house and you need a little time.

cards.example: Nói chậm chút được không? Em không hiểu.
cards.exampleMeaning: Can you speak a bit slower? I do not understand.
cards.brokenCue: A survival phrase. Very useful when speaking broken Vietnamese.
cards.notes: Pair this with asking the other person to speak slower.

cards.example: Anh nghe không kịp, nói chậm thôi.
cards.brokenCue: Useful when you want simple speech and less stress.
cards.notes: Direct and practical. Great for real conversation.
